knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Fishing in the moment — apply your strengths and trust instincts as you see different water, weather, pressure, and wildlife changes, then adapt instantly. Start with basics of where bass should be on seasonal migration, then apply search baits to find schools and choose the right lure to make them bite. Don't rely on past reports or history — conditions change overnight.'

KVD: 'First thing at a new lake: observe water clarity and check water temperature. Combine that with where bass should be according to seasonal patterns. The angler who figures out where bass are and how to catch them fastest wins. I'm not afraid to totally change technique, lure, and approach when conditions have changed.'
